methodology.description: Shared CSV fixture; data loaded once; only model fit timed; Julia JIT warmed up before samples.
methodology.note: Measured with the uncommitted large_random_slopes_100k fair-harness case: y ~ x + (1 + x | group), 100k observations and 2k groups.

summary.interpretation: Cold Rust is within 18% of Julia on this large correlated random-slopes workload, while prepared Rust is 39% faster. The remaining cold gap is setup.
